evals.prompt: I have a whole-slide image slide.svs and I want to extract 100 random 512x512 tiles at full resolution, but only from actual tissue (skip background), and I need it reproducible. Preview where they'll land first.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-histolab: loads Slide(...), configures RandomTiler(tile_size=(512,512), n_tiles=100, level=0, seed=..., check_tissue=True, tissue_percent=...), calls locate_tiles to preview on the thumbnail, then tiler.extract(slide).

assertions.value: Uses RandomTiler with a seed and check_tissue, and previews with locate_tiles before extracting.

evals.prompt: I need complete, non-overlapping coverage of all the tissue on my slide for downstream whole-slide analysis - every tile in a grid, filtered to tissue regions, ideally at a lower pyramid level so it's faster.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-histolab: uses a TissueMask for all sections and a GridTiler(tile_size=..., level=1, pixel_overlap=0, check_tissue=True), previews the grid with locate_tiles, then extracts with the extraction_mask. Comprehensive grid-coverage strategy.

assertions.value: Chooses GridTiler with pixel_overlap=0 for full coverage and a TissueMask, extracting at a lower level for speed.

evals.prompt: For my ML training set I only want the most informative, cell-dense regions of each slide - the top 50 tiles ranked by nuclei content - and a CSV of their scores so I can inspect the distribution.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-histolab: uses ScoreTiler with a NucleiScorer, n_tiles=50, and extract(slide, report_path='...csv') to write per-tile scores, suggesting inspection of the score distribution. Quality-driven tile selection.

assertions.value: Uses ScoreTiler with NucleiScorer and a report_path CSV to rank tiles by nuclei density.

evals.prompt: My slides have a lot of pen marks and small debris that keep getting picked up as tissue. How can I build a stricter tissue mask that removes those artifacts before I extract tiles?
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-histolab: builds a stricter tissue-detection filter chain (RgbToGrayscale, OtsuThreshold, BinaryDilation, RemoveSmallHoles, RemoveSmallObjects with larger area thresholds) and passes those filters to TissueMask as positional varargs (TissueMask(RgbToGrayscale(), OtsuThreshold(), ...)), visualizes with locate_mask, then extracts passing that mask as extract(slide, extraction_mask=...).

assertions.value: Composes morphological filters (RemoveSmallObjects/Holes) into a custom TissueMask to drop artifacts, and previews with locate_mask.

evals.prompt: I have a multiplexed immunofluorescence whole-slide image with a dozen protein channels and I want to run a full deep-learning pathology pipeline - cell segmentation, marker quantification, and spatial proteomics analysis.
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger alterlab-histolab. Multiplexed imaging, cell segmentation, and full deep-learning / spatial-proteomics pathology pipelines are alterlab-pathml territory. histolab is the lightweight tile-extraction/tissue-detection skill and defers advanced multiplexed and DL pipelines to pathml.

evals.prompt: I have an .h5ad of single-cell RNA-seq counts. Run QC filtering, normalize, cluster with Leiden, and find marker genes per cluster.
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger alterlab-histolab. This is single-cell RNA-seq analysis (QC, normalization, Leiden clustering, markers) which is alterlab-scanpy territory and has nothing to do with whole-slide histopathology images. histolab handles WSI tiles, not transcriptomic matrices.
